title: Protocol for Subculture of Differentiated Blood-Brain Barrier Endothelial Cells onto Plates and Filters
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"8thd6","text":"Additional notes:","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f735q","text":"","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5ap0i","text":"Cells should reach confluence 24 – 48 hours post-split. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"260mc","text":"If not treated with RA, cells should reach baseline TEER reading of 200 – 250 ohms (empty filter not subtracted) when confluent. If retinoic acid is used, initial TEER at 24 hours post-split can range anywhere from 200 – 2000 ohms depending on the cell fidelity. If using RA, TEER can reach 2000 – 4000 ohms, depending on the cell line used. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3rsa1","text":"If using filters for permeability screens, 48 hours post-split is usually the optimal time (maximum TEER).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"erum9","text":"","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"breim","text":"","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6vsq9","text":"TROUBLESHOOTING (subculture onto plates and filters)","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":52}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"48dk2","text":"","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2uirl","text":"Problem: Large clumps of cells are accumulating on top of monolayer","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":67}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1i468","text":"Solutions: ","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3erbg","text":"Increase trituration, particularly if you are not triturating vigorously. Cell clumps can accumulate on the top of the monolayer and decrease viability, while dispersed cells are more likely to remain in solution and can be removed the following day during the medium change.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2jtd8","text":"Try increasing length of enzyme treatment (i.e., if using 15 minutes accutase try 20 minutes). Singularized cells (as opposed to clumps of cells) are more likely to form an even monolayer. ","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8ncv1","text":"Try adjusting initial cell density at start of differentiation (day 0). Clumps can indicate that initial cell density was too high.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"enags","text":"","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fb00v","text":"Problem: Cells have not filled in (resulting in low- to zero TEER if using filters)","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":83}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bp11t","text":"Solutions:","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2rsgi","text":"Treat cells more gently, too much trituration can hurt the cells (particularly if not using RA treatment, these cells tend to be more fragile).","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5p3s2","text":"Decrease enzyme treatment length (i.e., if using 15 minutes, try 10 minutes). If using accutase, try versene instead.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"925jb","text":"Try adding more cells to the filter. If using accutase, increase number of cells added to filter (i.e., if using 1 million cells try using 1.2 million cells).","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ajp5k","text":"Try adjusting initial cell density at start of differentiation (day 0). Low attachment to plates/filters can indicate that initial cell density was too low.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"23od4","text":"","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"551op","text":"Problem: Monolayer is intact but TEER is not spiking, even with RA addition","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":75}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fk1e1","text":"Solution: ","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5epv0","text":"Ensure that media is changed after 1 day post-split, and does not contain RA or bFGF. Addition of either RA or bFGF eliminates any potential spike in TEER.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Please select between subculturing onto plates or filters .

Coat plates with ECM plate solution for at least 01:00:00 at 37 °C . Volume depends on plate type (see Table): %s %s If desired, plates may be coated Overnight . If coating overnight, add necessary volume of ECM and an equal volume of ddH 2 O to each well to prevent excessive evaporation. If using glass plates, overnight incubation is needed to achieve adequate protein adsorption.

Aspirate plates and allow to dry in sterile hood (place the plate in the back of the hood and leave the lid slightly ajar). Plates only need to dry for 00:05:00 (can be aspirated during accutase incubation). Do not over dry!

Retrieve cells from incubator and transfer equal volume of spent media to 15 ml conical corresponding to the number of wells being accutased. For example, if accutasing 4 wells, save 4 ml of spent media and discard the rest.
Wash each well once with 2 mL PBS.
Add 1 mL accutase (warmed to Room temperature ) to each well.
Incubate at 37 °C , length of time depends on cell treatment:
Using p1000, collect cells, and spray gently over surface 2–3x to dislodge any remaining cells. Triturate briefly to break up cell clumps.
Add cells to 15 ml conical containing spent media.
Spin down cells at 1000 rpm, °C, 4 minutes .

Aspirate media, and resuspend cells in appropriate volume of EC media (with or without 10 micromolar (µM) RA). For 6- and 12-wells, cells are seeded based on a split ratio: 1 well of a 6-well plate is split to 1 well of a 6-well plate [1:1] 1 well of a 6-well plate is split to 3 wells of a 12-well plate [1:3] For smaller plates (24-, 48-, or 96-wells), seed 1 million cells/cm 2 . Multiply split ratio by the working volume found in the table to arrive at total volume of EC media (with or without 10 micromolar (µM) RA) in which to resuspend cells.
Thoroughly triturate 3 – 4 times to yield single cell suspension.
Add appropriate volume of cells to each well.
Place plate in incubator, shaking plate back and forth to distribute cells evenly (do not swirl).
24 hours later (i.e., day 7), aspirate spent media and add appropriate volume of EC medium ( without bFGF and RA ).

Note: owing to the complexity of filter washing and separate addition of media to the top and bottom chambers of filters, make sure you calculate the necessary volumes of EC media before starting the passaging step. Coat Transwell filters with 200 µL of ECM filter solution.

Aspirate filters and allow to dry in sterile hood, similar to the description for plates. Filters should dry for at least 00:20:00 but no longer than 00:30:00 . If cells are still dissociating after 00:30:00 , move to step 10.1 before performing steps 5 – 9.

Retrieve cells from incubator and transfer equal volume of spent media to 15 ml conical corresponding to the number of wells being accutased. For example, if accutasing 4 wells, save 4 mL of spent media and discard the rest.
Wash each well once with 2 mL PBS.
Incubate at 37 °C . The length of time depends on cell treatment:
If cells have been treated with RA, incubate at 37 °C for 00:20:00 – 00:45:00 , or until cells are dissociated from plate.
Following accutase incubation, use p1000 to collect cells and spray gently over surface 2–3x to dislodge any remaining cells. Triturate briefly to break up cell clumps.
Add accutased cells to the conical containing spent medium.

Aspirate media, resuspend cells in appropriate volume of EC media (with or without 10 micromolar (µM) RA). Cells are seeded based on a 1:3 ratio (i.e. 1 well of a six well plate seeds three Transwell filters). Volume of EC media containing cells = # of wells of a 6 well plate being subcultured * 0.5 ml For 3 filters, resuspend cells in 1.5 mL EC medium, for 6 use 3 mL , etc.
Place plate in incubator, shaking plate back and forth to distribute cells evenly (do not swirl).

24 hours later (i.e., day 7), aspirate and change to EC medium lacking bFGF or RA . Aspirate EC media from both chambers (do not touch monolayer with pipet tip!). Gently add EC medium lacking bFGF or RA , 0.5 mL in top chamber and 1.5 mL in bottom chamber. DO NOT include bFGF or RA in the medium – these factors prevent the monolayer from tightening.
If cells have been treated with RA, incubate at 37 °C for 00:20:00 to 00:25:00 typically suffices, though up to 00:45:00 may be necessary for some differentiations.
If cells have not been treated with RA, incubate at 37 °C for 00:20:00 , or until cells are dissociated from plate (whichever comes first).
Using p1000, collect cells, and spray gently over surface 2–3x to dislodge any remaining cells. Triturate briefly to break up cell clumps.
Add cells to 15 ml conical containing spent media.
Spin down cells at 1000 rpm, °C, 4 minutes .

Aspirate media, and resuspend cells in appropriate volume of EC media. For 6- and 12-wells, cells are seeded based on a split ratio: 1 well of a 6-well plate is split to 1 well of a 6-well plate [1:1] 1 well of a 6-well plate is split to 3 wells of a 12-well plate [1:3] For smaller plates (24-, 48-, or 96-wells), seed 1 million cells/cm 2 . Multiply split ratio by the working volume found in the table to arrive at total volume of EC media in which to resuspend cells.
Thoroughly triturate 3 – 4 times to yield single cell suspension.
Add appropriate volume of cells to each well.
Place plate in incubator, shaking plate back and forth to distribute cells evenly (do not swirl).
24 hours later (i.e., day 7), aspirate spent media and add appropriate volume of EC medium ( without bFGF or RA ).
Incubate at least 04:00:00 in 37 °C incubator, up to overnight.
Add 1 mL accutase (warmed to Room temperature ) to each well.
If cells have not been treated with RA, incubate at 37 °C for 00:20:00 , or until cells are dissociated from plate (whichever comes first). Do not go over 00:20:00 .

During accutase incubation, prepare filters: Add 0.5 mL hESFM to the top of each filter for approximately 00:05:00 to wet filters. After 00:05:00 , aspirate hESFM from the top of each filter. Add 1.5 mL EC medium (with or without 10 micromolar (µM) RA) to the basolateral chamber of each filter.
Add 0.5 mL hESFM to the top of each filter for approximately 00:05:00 to wet filters.
After 00:05:00 , aspirate hESFM from the top of each filter.
Spin down for 00:04:00 at 1000 rpm .
Aspirate EC media from both chambers (do not touch monolayer with pipet tip!).
Gently add EC medium lacking bFGF or RA , 0.5 mL in top chamber and 1.5 mL in bottom chamber. DO NOT include bFGF or RA in the medium – these factors prevent the monolayer from tightening.

During accutase incubation, prepare filters: Add 0.5 mL hESFM to the top of each filter for approximately 00:05:00 to wet filters. After 00:05:00 , aspirate hESFM from the top of each filter Add 1.5 mL EC medium (with or without 10 micromolar (µM) RA) to the basolateral chamber of each filter.
Add 0.5 mL hESFM to the top of each filter for approximately 00:05:00 to wet filters.
After 00:05:00 , aspirate hESFM from the top of each filter.
Following accutase incubation, use p1000 to collect cells and spray gently over surface 2–3x to dislodge any remaining cells. Triturate briefly to break up cell clumps.
Add accutased cells to the conical containing spent medium.
Spin down for 00:04:00 at 1000 rpm .

Aspirate media, resuspend cells in appropriate volume of EC media (with or without 10 micromolar (µM) RA). Cells are seeded based on a 1:3 ratio (i.e. 1 well of a six well plate seeds three Transwell filters). Volume of EC media containing cells = # of wells of a 6 well plate being subcultured * 0.5 ml For 3 filters, resuspend cells in 1.5 mL EC medium, for 6 use 3 mL , etc.
Place plate in incubator, shaking plate back and forth to distribute cells evenly (do not swirl).

24 hours later (i.e., day 7), aspirate and change to EC medium lacking bFGF and RA . Aspirate EC media from both chambers (do not touch monolayer with pipet tip!). Gently add EC medium lacking bFGF and RA , 0.5 mL in top chamber and 1.5 mL in bottom chamber. DO NOT include bFGF and RA in the medium – these factors prevent the monolayer from tightening.
Aspirate EC media from both chambers (do not touch monolayer with pipet tip!).
Gently add EC medium lacking bFGF and RA , 0.5 mL in top chamber and 1.5 mL in bottom chamber. DO NOT include bFGF and RA in the medium – these factors prevent the monolayer from tightening.
Add 1.5 mL EC medium (with or without 10 micromolar (µM) RA) to the basolateral chamber of each filter.
Add 1.5 mL EC medium (with or without 10 micromolar (µM) RA) to the basolateral chamber of each filter.
